text: Hello? Hello? is this thing on? Are we live? Okay...
Good morning, people of America and the wonderful listeners of NSBNC!
I'm Jeronica Wilkins, reporting live, with what is the most consequential interview of our time.
With me, inside of a computer screen, is the President of our wonderful country
Mr. President, I'll be blunt. The people want answers.
Rumors are flying every which way, theorizing about your whereabouts.
So, lets put those conspiracies to rest. Where exactly are you right now?

links.label: I am stuck in a bunker.

text: You are safe. How reassuring.
Forgive me, Mr. President, but 'I am safe' doesn't exactly stop the presses.
Half this country hasn't slept in three days. The other half is stockpiling canned goods.
Saying you're safe tells us where you are, but not what you're doing there.
So let me ask it another way, since we're being simple tonight.
Are the rumors true, sir? Is the United States on the brink of military conflict?
Can't disclose your location. Naturally. I expected as much.
*leans into the microphone*
Though I must say, Mr. President, the very fact that your location is a secret speaks volumes.
In my twenty-two years behind this microphone, I have never had to interview a ghost.
The people filling up this station's switchboard, they don't need coordinates, sir.
They need to know their Commander in Chief is in command. Are you, Mr. President?
And are the rumors true, that we are going to war?
Excuse me?
I believe I misheard you, but did you say, that you are in a bunker?
So, then, we ARE in a tenuous situation, Mr. President?

links.label: No! I was just joking, haha

text: 'Exploring all options.'
That phrase, Mr. President, has been used to start more wars than I care to remember.
Our viewers are smart people. They know that 'all options' includes the ones no one says out loud.
Is military force among those options you're exploring, sir? A yes or no will do just fine.

links.label: I won't take anything off the table.

text: No conflict is imminent.
*scribbles notes furiously*
Now THAT is something, Mr. President. Our listeners will want to hear more on that.
Because just this morning, multiple sources within the Pentagon have told us otherwise.
Employees have come out and spoke of 'inevitible' conflict.
So where exactly is the disconnect, sir? Between what you're telling me and what
your own government appears to be telling anyone who will listen?
I see.
Mr. President, I have interviewed five sitting presidents. I know what a 'no comment' sounds like.
And I know what it means.
It means yes. We are in a major conflict.
So let me give you an opportunity to clarify for the American people before this broadcast ends.
Because they are going to hear that silence. And they are going to draw their own conclusions.
Is there anything at all you'd like to say to a nation that is very, very afraid right now?

links.label: America is strong. We will endure.
Trust the process. Trust your government.

text: Nothing off the table!
Ladies and gentlemen, you heard that directly from the President of the United States.
Nothing. Off. The table.
Mr. President, does 'nothing off the table' include a first strike?
That is something the country can hold onto tonight, Mr. President. Truly.
But 'last resort' implies there are steps before it. First, second, third resort.
Walk us through them, sir. What are your plans when in talks with Russia?
Of course not. And yet, sir, the leaks speak rather loudly on their own.
I digress. Regardless of who or who is telling the truth,
The American people to be ensured that they will be safe, not matter what happens
In regards to Russia, where would you place our relations to them?
The people are tired of being scared, Mr. President. They want some good news.
According to my informant, whom you refuse to acknowledge, you plan on talking to
Russia's leader Katerina Romanov. Is this true?
Regardless of who or who is telling the truth,
I believe that this provides an even bigger concern
Because somebody in that building is saying one thing to the press and something very different to you.
Doesn't that concern you, Mr. President? That your own house may not be in order?

links.label: I couldn't care less about some useless leak.

text: Oh! How...to the point.
Well, then.
As you have just confirmed our involvement in this world's ongoing chaos
Is military force going to be included in this, sir?
Will you be sending innocent young lives to fight for this country?

links.label: I won't take anything off the table.

text: Sir, as funny as you think you are, right now is not the time for jokes.
Let me ask you again. Where exactly are you right now?

links.label: I jest, I'm in a bunker

text: I see
Well, Mr. President, it seems you have...
...answered, in a way, all of my questions!
Is there anything you wish to share to the American people, if at all?
The people need your reassurance

links.label: America is strong. We will endure.
Trust the process. Trust your government.

text: Full confidence in your team, is that correct?
Mr. President, the American people didn't elect your team to provide information to them.
That's what they elected you for
With all due respect, sir, what's more important is that people trust you.
If there something that you are purposely hiding from us,
I think the country deserves to know that too.
Wow
How ... inspiring! I'm speachless, sir!
Moving on, rumors have been circulating
of you having future talks with Russia's leader Katerina Romanov, is this true?
And that, Mr. President, is something I hope is true.
Well, it seems like we are out of time! Is there anything else you would like to say,
especially for those watching at home?

links.label: Trust the process. Trust your government.

text: Hello?
Did we cut out? Did the terminal cut out?
Mr. President, I'm not sure if you heard properly, what is your status?
All Right Folks. That's all we have today for our interview.
Thank you so much, Mr.President
for speaking on these delicate matters.
This is Jeronica Wilkins, reporting live.
Stay safe out there, folks.
All Right Folks! That's all we have today for our interview.
Thank you so much, Mr.President,
for spending your precious time to speak on these matters.
This is Jeronica Wilkins, reporting live, and signing out.
That's great to hear!
Ladies and gentlemen, we are at the table. The President has confirmed it.
That is the best possible news this broadcast could deliver tonight. Bar none.
Keep talking, Mr. President. Whatever it takes, keep talking.
Jeronica Wilkins, NSBNC. Goodnight.
Oh dear,
Moving on to my next question,
What, as of now, are our plans to resolve this conflict. is peace possible?
